commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d93528f4cbf6b173e2895ff11d3bfbff57a8b137)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates memory-core status identity comparison and regression tests so local memory indexes using `local.modelPath` can reopen clean instead of staying dirty.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +36. Total +41 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source and linked issue evidence: current main's plain status path compares against `memorySearch.model`/adapter default while the local provider writes `local.modelPath`; I did not run a live GGUF repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor live behavior proof plus maintainer review of the alias-safe fix shape; ClawSweeper should not queue automated repair while proof is still mock-only.

target.title: fix(memory): honor local model path in index identity
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes local memory-search indexes being reported dirty after a rebuild when `agents.*.memorySearch.provider = "local"` uses a configured `local.modelPath`.

Why does this matter now?

- #91001 reports that `openclaw memory status` still shows `Dirty: yes` after `openclaw memory index --force` with local embeddings, leaving vector search paused even though the index was rebuilt.

What is the intended outcome?

- Status-only memory managers compare index identity against the same effective local model path that the local embedding provider writes into index metadata.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No local embedding runtime, node-llama-cpp, vector-store, or CLI formatting behavior changes.

What does success look like?

- A rebuilt local index using `local.modelPath` reports a valid index identity and `dirty: false` when reopened through the status path before provider initialization.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The status-only identity comparison in `manager-sync-ops.ts` and the regression coverage in `index.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91001

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90413

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request found; issue is labeled `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, and `clawsweeper:source-repro`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: status-only memory index identity checks used `memorySearch.model`/adapter defaults for local embeddings even though the local provider records `local.modelPath` as the indexed model.
- Real environment tested: local source checkout in `/tmp/openclaw-91001`, Node `v24.16.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, SQLite-backed memory manager test harness.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t '#91001'`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix:
  - Named regression: `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed | 38 skipped (39); Duration 7.38s`
  - Full file: `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 39 passed (39); Duration 9.48s`
- Observed result after fix: the new `#91001` regression indexes with `provider: "local"` and `local.modelPath = "/models/embeddinggemma-300M-Q8_0.gguf"`, reopens a status-only manager, and observes `status.dirty === false` plus `status.custom.indexIdentity.status === "valid"`.
- What was not tested: a live `openclaw memory index --force` run with node-llama-cpp and a real GGUF model file.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: source CLI proof was attempted with `pnpm exec openclaw memory --help`, but this shallow checkout did not have `@openclaw/normalization-core` linked under `node_modules`, so plugin CLI loading failed before `memory` commands could run. The regression exercises the real memory manager and SQLite identity path without loading the optional local embedding runtime.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #91001 shows the before behavior: after local rebuild, `Dirty: yes` and index identity reported a built/expected mismatch or missing metadata.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t '#91001'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added `keeps status clean when local modelPath is the indexed model (#91001)` to `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A status-only manager expected the adapter/configured model while metadata stored the local provider's effective `local.modelPath`, so status could report the rebuilt index dirty.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Memory index identity status for local embeddings.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The change is scoped to the status/identity expectation for `provider: "local"` with `local.modelPath`; existing provider-initialized behavior is unchanged, and the full memory index test file passes.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and, if maintainers require it, live CLI proof on a machine with node-llama-cpp plus a real local GGUF model.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- None yet.
target.author: stevenepalmer

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
